report_name: Tata Electronics Confirms Cyberattack as 'World Leaks' Group Dumps Alleged Apple, Tesla Data

threat_actors.name: World Leaks
threat_actors.description: A data extortion group, believed to be a rebrand of the Hunters International ransomware operation, that claimed responsibility for the Tata Electronics data breach. The group stole 630GB of data and published it on the dark web, focusing on data theft and extortion rather than encryption.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft and extortion for financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Archive via Utility
attack_patterns.description: The attackers staged and compressed large volumes of data using archiving utilities before exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor achieved impact by publicly releasing sensitive stolen data after a failed ransom negotiation, rather than by encrypting it on victim systems.

tools.name: 7-Zip
tools.description: The 7-Zip archiving utility was likely used by the attackers to stage and compress data before exfiltration, as indicated by hunting hints for `7z.exe`.

tools.name: RAR
tools.description: The RAR archiving utility was likely used by the attackers to stage and compress data before exfiltration, as indicated by hunting hints for `rar.exe`.

identities.name: Tata Electronics
identities.description: A major Indian subsidiary of the Tata Group and a critical component supplier for Apple and Tesla, who was the primary victim of the cyberattack and data breach.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: A key client of Tata Electronics whose sensitive data, including iPhone component designs and quality inspection documents, was leaked as a result of the supply chain attack.

identities.name: Tesla
identities.description: A key client of Tata Electronics whose sensitive data, including engineering drawings for the Model 3, was leaked as a result of the supply chain attack.

identities.name: TSMC
identities.description: A client of Tata Electronics whose data may have been included in the leak.

identities.name: Qualcomm
identities.description: A client of Tata Electronics whose data may have been included in the leak.

infrastructure.name: World Leaks Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the World Leaks group to publish the over 630GB of data stolen from Tata Electronics.

indicators.name: Suspicious Archiving Tool Execution on Servers
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Archiving Tool Execution on Servers
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e4f-4b1a-9c8d-7e6f5a4b3c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of common archiving tools (7z.exe, rar.exe) on server systems, which could indicate data staging prior to exfiltration.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-29
references:
  - https://www.thaicert.or.th/en/2026/06/25/tata-electronics-confirms-cyberattack-after-world-leaks-claims-theft-and-publication-of-company-data/
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\rar.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the use of common archiving tools like 7z.exe and rar.exe, which is a common data staging technique used by attackers before exfiltration.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Archiving Tool Execution on Servers
Suspicious Archiving Tool Execution on Servers
